front.title: A small, strange book
front.description: A small manual written on what appears to be sheets of supple leaves. It is written in the common script but in a strangely poetic sequence of nonsense syllables.

content.text: The manual reveals the recipe for an elderberry wine that brims with Fae magic, but to learn the recipe, you either...
… need help from a Fae poet, or perhaps one of the Forest Folk; or...
… risk getting the recipe wrong, poisoning or cursing whoever drinks the wine you brew.

back.title: Old Mother’s Wine
back.description:
When you **_sacrifice a memory to a berry- laden elder_** **_tree_**, tell us what you gave up. Mark 1 lost memory and roll 1d6; if you roll less than your lost memories, change your instinct and clear your marks.

When you **_harvest the berries and spend a season_** **_making wine from them_**, gain a skin of Old Mother’s Wine (5 uses, magical).

When you **_get tipsy on Old Mother’s Wine_**, expend a use. For a night and a day, your eyes are open to Fae magic, meaning:

- You see the truth behind Fae illusions and glamours
- You are immune to Fae enchantments
- You can freely enter Fae domains and walk the Fae paths
- Time passes normally for you while in Fae domains
